sources.title: Proposed PFAS Rescission Rule
sources.supports: EPA proposes rescinding MCLs/regulatory determinations for PFHxS, PFNA, GenX (HFPO-DA), and PFBS Hazard Index; final action intended 2026.

sources.title: Rescission of Regulatory Determinations ... Four PFAS Substances (2026-10085)
sources.supports: Formal proposal text; 4 of 6 regulated PFAS dropped from NPDWR scope if finalized.

sources.title: Proposed PFOA and PFOS Compliance Extension Rule
sources.supports: Optional 2-year extension of PFOA/PFOS MCL compliance from 2029 to 2031; comments to 2026-07-20, hearing 2026-07-07. Spring-2026 'final' rule did NOT land as final.

sources.title: EPA Announces It Will Keep Maximum Contaminant Levels for PFOA, PFOS
sources.supports: PFOA/PFOS MCLs retained - the residual demand driver.

sources.title: PFAS in Drinking Water tracker
sources.supports: Litigation/rulemaking timeline context; regulatory uncertainty through 2026-2027.

sources.title: Competition to destroy 'forever chemicals' heats up
sources.supports: Crowded destruction field: SCWO, HALT, electrochemical, plasma all racing; DoD demo lineup.

sources.title: DOD-funded PFAS demo projects show promise for remediation and destruction
sources.supports: DoD large-scale demos: Aquagga (HALT) + three full-scale SCWO teams (Revive, General Atomics, Arcadis/374Water/Clean Earth). Plasma absent from this flagship lineup.

sources.title: DoD ESTCP + DIU partner to advance remediation of PFAS
sources.supports: DoD procurement flows through ESTCP/DIU prototype projects launched Nov 2023 - slots already awarded.

sources.title: U.S. Air Force awards Onvector Phase II SBIR / STRATFI + Phase III OT
sources.supports: Onvector holds AFWERX Phase II ($1.25M, 2023), Strategic Funding Increase, and Phase III Other Transaction to deploy Plasma Vortex on AF bases - the exact DoD plasma slot V02 targets.

sources.title: Onvector awarded second $1M DoD ESTCP contract (Joint Base Cape Cod)
sources.supports: Onvector already at full-scale multi-stream ESTCP demonstration stage.

sources.title: 2025 PFAS destruction in review: from pilots to commercial execution
sources.supports: Revive (Battelle spinout) in permitted commercial service; largest state AFFF take-back program (New Jersey); WEFTEC 2025 Innovative Technology Award.

sources.title: PFAS Annihilator treats 160,000 gal/day landfill leachate at >99.99% destruction
sources.supports: SCWO already at utility-relevant continuous throughput - erodes the plasma throughput pitch.

sources.title: 374Water Q1 2026 financial results
sources.supports: FY2025 revenue $215K (-52% YoY), cash fell $10.7M to $0.9M, Nasdaq bid-price notice; Q1-2026 revenue $551K. Public-company proof that destruction WTP is thin and slow.

sources.title: Aclarity secures $16M Series A for electrochemical PFAS destruction
sources.supports: Funded electrochemical rival in commercial rollout since Feb 2024.

sources.title: Oxyle raises $16M; first commercial installation, multi-year treatment contracts
sources.supports: Catalytic destruction rival with revenue-generating pilots, contracts 2025+.

sources.title: Aquagga - PFAS destruction units (HALT)
sources.supports: HALT rival with EPA SBIRs and a DoD large-scale demo slot; lower energy than SCWO.

sources.title: Gradiant ForeverGone sets new standard for PFAS destruction (Munich Airport)
sources.supports: Water-tech unicorn sells integrated foam-fractionation + electrooxidation (concentrate AND destroy); 100x footprint cut on Destruction Engine; AFFF-site deployment. Missed by one-pager; owns the exact 'polishing after concentration' slot.

sources.title: Reactor design in plasma-liquid systems for wastewater treatment (2025 review)
sources.supports: Reactor design/mass transfer of plasma species to liquid is the critical performance limiter - not PSU electrical efficiency.

sources.title: PFAS remediation by commercially available pulsed plasma technology
sources.supports: Pulsed corona at 20-24 kWh/m3 for long-chain PFAS; energy-per-volume set by reactor architecture (20-330 kWh/m3 spread across designs).

sources.title: Enhancing interface reactions by microbubbles for PFOA decomposition
sources.supports: PFAS are surfactants; destruction rate scales with gas-liquid interfacial area/enrichment - mass-transfer physics.

sources.title: DoD PFAS cleanup status (723 installations; CERCLA pace)
sources.supports: PA/SI alone 1-3 years; CERCLA steps take years-decades; AFFF fluorinated-foam waiver extended to 2026-10-01. DoD demand is real but slow and demo-gated.

sources.title: GAO-25-107401: DOD needs to provide Congress more information on PFAS costs
sources.supports: Congressional scrutiny of DoD PFAS cost/pace - procurement caution, not a fast wave.

sources.title: Eagle Harbor Technologies - precision pulsed power incl. water treatment
sources.supports: Merchant precision pulsed-power supplier already serving low-temp plasma and water treatment - incumbents can buy the 'differentiator'.

sources.title: Transient Plasma Systems - nanosecond pulsed power products
sources.supports: Second merchant nanosecond pulsed-power vendor; PSU whitespace claim overstated.

sources.title: USTC low-temperature (40-60C) catalytic total defluorination of PFAS (Nature)
sources.supports: Chinese activity is academic chemistry (potential leapfrog route), no commercial plasma-PFAS vendor surfaced - consistent with one-pager, but a substitution threat.
